Juncus is a genus of flowering plants in the sedge family (Juncaceae), commonly known as rushes. The health benefits of Juncus species include their potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Juncus species include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Juncus species include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
